headline: Behavioral training and tool discipline emerged as the week's defining theme, with small finetuned models outperforming far larger ones on structured tasks.
body: Snorkel showed a 4B model finetuned with reinforcement learning for tool discipline beating Qwen 3 235B on structured query tasks, self-correcting where the larger model hallucinated, reinforcing that post-training and behavioral training now outweigh raw scale for certain task classes. This aligns with new technique entrants including post-training, precision metrics, trust-region control, and curriculum, alongside Gemma 4's 31B open model ranking fourth on LM Arena while running on single GPUs and phones. AI-native coding tooling moved in parallel, with Cursor scaling to 700 people and over 60% of Fortune 500 coverage, and design system tooling rotating up via Claude Design positioned as a Figma replacement. Security and verification pressures surfaced through prompt injection, AI worms, and lawyers sanctioned for AI-generated citations to nonexistent cases.
